testcase.name: test_timeout
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_cache.TestUnpywallCache object at 0x118471350> example_cache = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x1184afb50>
def test_timeout(self, example_cache): timeout = 1 doi = '10.1016/j.jns.2020.116832' example_cache.timeout = timeout example_cache.delete(doi) assert doi not in example_cache.content > example_cache.get(doi)
tests/test_cache.py:61: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ unpywall/cache.py:115: in get downloaded = self.download(doi, errors) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ unpywall/cache.py:188: in download raise e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x1184afb50> doi = '10.1016/j.jns.2020.116832', errors = 'raise'
def download(self, doi: str, errors: str): """ Retrieve a record from Unpaywall.
Parameters ---------- doi : str The DOI to be retrieved. errors : str Whether to ignore or raise errors. """ from .utils import UnpywallURL
mandatory_wait_time = int(os.environ.get('MANDATORY_WAIT_TIME', 1)) time.sleep(mandatory_wait_time) url = UnpywallURL(doi=doi).doi_url
try: r = treq.get(url) > if r.status_code != 200: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'status_code'
unpywall/cache.py:180: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_get

failure.message: AttributeError: module 'treq' has no attribute 'Response'. Did you mean: 'response'?

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_cache.TestUnpywallCache object at 0x1184710f0> example_cache = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x1185fa6d0> backup_cache = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x1185faa50>
def test_get(self, example_cache, backup_cache): doi = '10.1016/j.jns.2020.116832' example_cache.delete(doi) assert doi not in example_cache.content assert doi not in example_cache.access_times > assert isinstance(backup_cache.get(doi), treq.Response) # Changed to treq.Response ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: module 'treq' has no attribute 'Response'. Did you mean: 'response'?
tests/test_cache.py:70: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_download
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_cache.TestUnpywallCache object at 0x1184d4af0> example_cache = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x11879bed0>
def test_download(self, example_cache): doi = '10.1016/j.jns.2020.116832' > assert isinstance(example_cache.download(doi, 'raise'), treq.Response) # Changed to treq.Response ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_cache.py:91: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ unpywall/cache.py:188: in download raise e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <unpywall.cache.UnpywallCache object at 0x11879bed0> doi = '10.1016/j.jns.2020.116832', errors = 'raise'
def download(self, doi: str, errors: str): """ Retrieve a record from Unpaywall.
Parameters ---------- doi : str The DOI to be retrieved. errors : str Whether to ignore or raise errors. """ from .utils import UnpywallURL
mandatory_wait_time = int(os.environ.get('MANDATORY_WAIT_TIME', 1)) time.sleep(mandatory_wait_time) url = UnpywallURL(doi=doi).doi_url
try: r = treq.get(url) > if r.status_code != 200: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'status_code'
unpywall/cache.py:180: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_query
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_unpywall.TestUnpywall object at 0x11842b9b0> Unpywall = <class 'unpywall.Unpywall'>
def test_query(self, Unpywall):
> df = Unpywall.query(query='test', is_oa=True, errors='ignore')
tests/test_unpywall.py:102: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
query = 'test', is_oa = True, format = 'raw', errors = 'ignore'
@staticmethod def query(query: str, is_oa: bool = False, format: str = 'raw', errors: str = 'raise') -> pd.DataFrame: """ Parses information for a given query from the Unpaywall API service and returns it as a pandas DataFrame.
Parameters ---------- query : str The text to search for. is_oa : bool A boolean value indicating whether the returned records should be Open Access or not. format: str The format of the DataFrame. errors : str Either 'raise' or 'ignore'. If the parameter errors is set to 'ignore' than errors will not raise an exception.
Returns ------- DataFrame A pandas DataFrame that contains information from the Unpaywall API service. """
data = Unpywall.get_json(query=query, is_oa=is_oa, errors=errors)
df = pd.DataFrame()
> for obj in data['results']: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
unpywall/__init__.py:207: TypeError

testcase.name: test_get_json
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_unpywall.TestUnpywall object at 0x1184d5ae0> Unpywall = <class 'unpywall.Unpywall'>
def test_get_json(self, Unpywall):
> with pytest.raises(treq.exceptions.HTTPError): ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: module 'treq' has no attribute 'exceptions'
tests/test_unpywall.py:110: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_download_pdf_handle
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_unpywall.TestUnpywall object at 0x1184a3f20> Unpywall = <class 'unpywall.Unpywall'>
def test_download_pdf_handle(self, Unpywall):
assert isinstance( > Unpywall.download_pdf_handle('10.1038/nature12373'), BytesIO) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_unpywall.py:147: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
doi = '10.1038/nature12373'
@staticmethod def download_pdf_handle(doi: str) -> BytesIO: """ This function returns a file-like object containing the requested PDF.
Parameters ---------- doi : str The DOI of the requested paper.
Returns ------- BytesIO The handle of the PDF file. """ pdf_link = Unpywall.get_pdf_link(doi) r = treq.get(pdf_link) > return BytesIO(bytearray(r.text, encoding='utf-8')) ^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'text'
unpywall/__init__.py:425: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_download_pdf_file

failure.message: TypeError: HTTPClient.request() got an unexpected keyword argument 'stream'

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_unpywall.TestUnpywall object at 0x11855faf0> Unpywall = <class 'unpywall.Unpywall'> capfd = <_pytest.capture.CaptureFixture object at 0x11890d480>
def test_download_pdf_file(self, Unpywall, capfd):
filename = 'test.pdf' filepath = './test_dir'
> Unpywall.download_pdf_file('10.1038/nature12373', filename=filename, filepath=filepath, progress=True)
tests/test_unpywall.py:162: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ unpywall/__init__.py:495: in download_pdf_file r = treq.get(url, stream=url) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/api.py:23: in get return _client(kwargs).get(url, headers=headers, _stacklevel=4, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <treq.client.HTTPClient object at 0x1187dba50> url = 'https://dash.harvard.edu/bitstream/1/12285462/1/Nanometer-Scale%20Thermometry.pdf' kwargs = {'_stacklevel': 4, 'headers': None, 'stream': 'https://dash.harvard.edu/bitstream/1/12285462/1/Nanometer-Scale%20Thermometry.pdf'}
def get(self, url: _URLType, **kwargs: Any) -> "Deferred[_Response]": """ See :func:`treq.get()`. """ kwargs.setdefault("_stacklevel", 3) > return self.request("GET", url, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: HTTPClient.request() got an unexpected keyword argument 'stream'
.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/treq/client.py:151: TypeError
